title: log-driver=passthrough issue with /dev/stderr
body:
```
ansible@chuwi:~$ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.2.1
API Version:  4.2.1
Go Version:   go1.19
Built:        Wed Dec 31 18:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```
/kind bug

**Description**

I have been migrating my SystemD unit files to reference the new `--log-driver=passthrough` option.
It has been working rather well, except for this one container that logs to `/dev/stderr`.
I receive the following error when it attempts to write to the error log which is in turn symlinked to stderr.
```
(6)No such device or address: AH00091: apache2: could not open error log file /var/log/apache2/error.log.
AH00015: Unable to open logs
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

This is my unit file and I have tested without the security options at the end.
```
## ANSIBLE MANAGED
[Unit]
Description=Podman librespeed.service
Documentation=man:podman-run(1)
Documentation=https://access.redhat.com/documentation/en-us/red_hat_enterprise_linux/8/html/building_running_and_managing_containers/assembly_porting-containers-to-systemd-using-podman_building-running-and-managing-containers
Wants=network-online.target
After=network-online.target
RequiresMountsFor=%t/containers

[Service]
Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
ExecStartPre=-/usr/bin/podman rm -f --ignore --cidfile=%t/%n.ctr-id
ExecStartPre=-/bin/rm -f %t/%n.ctr-id
ExecStartPre=-/bin/rm -f %t/%n.pid
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run \
    --name librespeed \
    -d \
    --cidfile=%t/%n.ctr-id --sdnotify=conmon --cgroups=no-conmon \
    --conmon-pidfile=%t/%n.pid \
    --network slirp4netns:allow_host_loopback=true,enable_ipv6=true \
    --restart no \
    --replace \
    --rm \
    --stop-timeout 60 \
    --env "MODE=standalone" \
    --env "TITLE=Speedtest" \
    --env "TELEMETRY=True" \
    --env "REDACT_IP_ADDRESS=False" \
    --env "PASSWORD=admin" \
    --env "EMAIL=root@localhost" \
    --env "DISTANCE=mi" \
    --env "WEBPORT=8080" \
    -p 8001:8080 \
    -v /etc/localtime:/etc/localtime:ro \
    -v %E/librespeed/:/database/:rw \
    -l=diun.enable \
    --cap-drop=all \
    --security-opt=no-new-privileges \
    --cap-add=chown \
    docker.io/adolfintel/speedtest:latest
ExecReload=-/usr/bin/podman stop --ignore --cidfile=%t/%n.ctr-id
ExecReload=-/usr/bin/podman rm -f --ignore --cidfile=%t/%n.ctr-id
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman stop --ignore --cidfile=%t/%n.ctr-id
ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman rm -f --ignore --cidfile=%t/%n.ctr-id
Type=notify
NotifyAccess=all
KillMode=mixed
PIDFile=%t/%n.pid
Restart=on-failure
SyslogIdentifier=%N

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
WantedBy=default.target
```

**Describe the results you received:**
Apache could not log to stderr.

**Describe the results you expected:**
Apache should be able to log to stderr.

I am using the Alvistack Podman Repo to get the latest builds on Debian.

**Output of `podman info`:**
<details>

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
ansible@chuwi:~$ apt list podman
Listing... Done
podman/unknown,now 100:4.2.1-1 amd64 [installed]
N: There are 4 additional versions. Please use the '-a' switch to see them.
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Physical Debian 11 Bullseye

comments.body:
To sure of what is failing, you could try to `strace` the container process and see what syscall is failing.  I think it is  attempting to open the `/dev/stderr` file and that fails because stderr is a socket under systemd.

I am closing the issue as I don't think there is anything doable in Podman, since it is an error caused by the interaction of the container payload with systemd, but feel free to comment further.
